evals.prompt: Check the status of all my automation systems. Are they all running?
evals.expected_output: Runs the ecosystem status script and reports the health of each system
expectations: Runs ~/scripts/ecosystem_status.py or ~/scripts/status
Reports status for multiple automation systems
Shows running/idle/stale/down status for each system
Flags any systems needing attention
Presents results in a dashboard or summary format

evals.prompt: Is my tax organizer running? What about the Monarch Money server?
evals.expected_output: Status check focused on the specific systems asked about
expectations: Runs the ecosystem status check script
Specifically reports on the Tax PDF Organizer status
Specifically reports on the Monarch Money MCP Server status
Indicates whether each is running, idle, or down
Does not confuse status checking with configuration

evals.prompt: I need to add my Notion API token to the ecosystem config. How do I set it up?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for adding Notion API token to ~/scripts/ecosystem.env
expectations: References ~/scripts/ecosystem.env as the central config file
Shows the correct environment variable name for Notion token (NOTION_TOKEN or similar)
Explains how to source the env file from ~/.zshrc
Does not suggest editing ~/.zshrc directly for individual keys
Mentions the ecosystem.env.example file if applicable

evals.prompt: Set up my Pushover credentials for notifications. My user key is 'test-user-key' and my app token is 'test-app-token'.
expectations: Writes to ~/scripts/ecosystem.env (not ~/.zshrc directly)
Sets PUSHOVER_USER_KEY and PUSHOVER_APP_TOKEN variables
Uses the provided credential values
Advises reloading the shell (source ~/.zshrc) after changes
Does not expose credentials in any insecure way

evals.prompt: Add push notification support to a Python script that monitors a folder for new files. When a new file appears, it should send a Pushover notification with the filename and size.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with Pushover notification integration using the ecosystem's notify.py module
expectations: Imports or references ~/scripts/notify.py send_notification() function
Uses Pushover as the notification backend (not just macOS notifications)
Sends notification with the filename included in the message
Sets an appropriate priority level (0 or 1 for file monitoring)
Reads PUSHOVER_USER_KEY and PUSHOVER_APP_TOKEN from environment

evals.prompt: Send a test notification to verify my Pushover setup is working. Use priority 0 (normal) and include a test message.
evals.expected_output: A notification sent via Pushover with confirmation of delivery
expectations: Uses the send_notification() function from ~/scripts/notify.py
Sets priority to 0 (normal)
Includes a descriptive test message
Checks for required environment variables before sending
Reports success or failure of the notification delivery
